fedEvents.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.title: Trump bought as much as $5 million in Axon stock before ICE sought $220 million Taser deal
fedEvents.content: Axon Enterprise has ramped up lobbying in Congress on federal law enforcement technology as ICE pursues a potential $220 million Taser deal.

fedEvents.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
fedEvents.content: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.

fedEvents.title: Supreme Court rulings on Fed, FTC: What they mean for consumers
fedEvents.content: The Supreme Court issued a pair of highly anticipated rulings on Monday that have far-reaching implications for household finances.

fedEvents.title: Trump laments 'tremendous loss' on mail-in ballots at Supreme Court, doubles down on voter-ID bill
fedEvents.content: Justice Amy Coney Barrett, whom Trump nominated in 2020, rejected arguments that federal laws preempt Mississippi permitting late-arriving absentee ballots.

items.title: Trump’s weekend Iran strikes keep sparking Monday stock rallies. Here’s what the data shows us.
items.content: Call it the ‘Axios put’: On average, stocks have been up more on Mondays in the second quarter than in recent years

items.title: ‘I don’t think I’ll make it to 80’: I’m 70 and single. Do I take out a reverse mortgage or a home-equity agreement?
items.content: “I’m torn between these two options.”

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.content: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”

items.title: Rocket Lab to take on SpaceX’s Starlink with $8 billion acquisition
items.content: Rocket Lab said it is buying Iridium as a “shortcut” to expanding its growing capabilities.

items.title: Tesla’s stock rips higher after a long-awaited update to self-driving technology
items.content: EV maker’s stock has best day in over a year, but is still down for the month.

items.title: Charter may be making ‘frenemies’ with SpaceX, and its stock is soaring
items.content: Shares of other telecoms are falling, as SpaceX’s Starlink has options to move into the wireless market. A potential partnership with Charter could be one of those options.

items.title: Most Americans have no idea how close we just came to financial chaos
items.content: If you care about your money, close your eyes and say a prayer of thanks to John Roberts and Brett Kavanaugh

items.title: Value stocks beat growth when inflation is high. Here are 13 stocks top newsletters are betting on now.
items.content: Many portfolio experts are wrong about what drives value outperformance. This single metric explains why.

items.title: Bye-bye, ‘HODL’: Strategy now has a plan to sell bitcoin to raise cash and buy its stock
items.content: Strategy disclosed a program to sell bitcoin “from time to time” to fund its U.S. dollar reserve and share repurchases.

items.title: My sister died and I’m leaving New York to help raise my niece in Colorado. At 33, am I making a financial mistake?
items.content: “I need to decide what I can afford when it comes to storing the belongings from my studio apartment.”

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.content: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
items.content: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

items.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
items.content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
items.content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

items.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

items.content: Maverick Natural Resources has operations in Texas and Oklahoma.

items.title: Chinese Investors With Few Options Turn to Dividends
items.content: Companies with good payouts are now the hottest bet in China markets.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall But Direction for Long-End Yields Still Seen Upward
items.content: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield fell and ING said the the long end of the Treasury curve will continue trading at higher yields even as Trump hasn’t delivered anything to shock markets so far.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says
items.content: Oil edged lower in early trade as Goldman Sachs said the latest round of U.S. sanctions against Russia haven’t had a significant impact on the country’s oil exports.

items.title: The Extra Reward for Owning Stocks Over Bonds Has Disappeared
items.content: There is little sign of crimped demand for equities among individual investors, who remain bullish after two years of blockbuster gains.

items.content: The president has told Bank of America and JPMorgan Chase to stop cutting conservatives off from doing business.

items.title: Canada to Review Amazon Contracts After Quebec Layoffs
items.content: Canada has warned Amazon.com that it is reviewing business ties with the company’s cloud-computing unit following Amazon’s decision to shut down its warehouses in Quebec, leading to the layoff of 1,700 workers.

items.title: How People Actually Make a Living on TikTok
items.content: The platform has provided a surge in discovery and revenue for companies that may not be able to replicate it elsewhere.

items.title: What Happens Now That TikTok's Gone Dark?
items.content: The company told users the app isn’t available and told them to stay tuned. Trump said he will likely intervene.

items.title: How a Little-Known Finnish Company Became One of the World's Hottest Gadget Startups
items.content: Oura’s smart ring measures heart health and can predict when you may be getting sick

items.title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
items.content: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

items.title: China factory activity grows faster than expected in June on tech export demand
items.content: China's manufacturing engine held up this year as surging demand for artificial intelligence technology helped offset the drag from Middle East turmoil.

items.content: Axon Enterprise has ramped up lobbying in Congress on federal law enforcement technology as ICE pursues a potential $220 million Taser deal.

items.title: Australia's competition regulator takes Amazon to court over alleged unfair Prime subscription contract terms
items.content: Australia's competition regulator is taking Amazon to court, alleging its Prime contracts required subscribers to pay AU$2.99 to avoid advertising, with no option for refunds

items.title: Short sellers keep betting against Pop Mart — even though it's been a losing trade
items.content: Short sellers are doubling down on Pop Mart International even as a recent share price recovery turns their bearish bets on the Chinese toymaker increasingly risky.

items.title: Eli Lilly, Regeneron among first companies selected for FDA initiative to speed review of new manufacturing facilities
items.content: Eli Lilly and Regeneron are among the first seven companies the FDA has selected for its PreCheck Pilot Program, CNBC has learned.

items.title: Supreme Court lets presidents fire independent regulators, rules for Trump in FTC case
items.content: The ruling allowing President Donald Trump to fire FTC Commissioner Rebecca Slaughter overturns a key precedent known as "Humphrey’s Executor."

items.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
items.content: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

items.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
items.content: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.

items.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: U.S.-China tech rivalry heats up—in other countries
items.content: U.S. and Chinese tech companies are increasingly pursuing opportunities outside their home markets, with government policy support.

items.title: Apple supplier Luxshare seeks up to $3.1 billion in Hong Kong share sale
items.content: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry is seeking to raise as much as $24.27 billion Hong Kong dollars ($3.1 billion) through a Hong Kong share sale.

items.title: SpaceX bulls are back betting on huge, fast gains as Rocket Lab surges
AeroVironment soars 19% on earnings beat, backlog grows to $1.2 billion
items.content: Dronemaker AeroVironment's autonomous system revenue beat expectations by $90 million and its backlog continued to grow.

items.title: Industry lobbyists push House committee to block a ban on Pentagon defense contractors buying back their own stock
items.content: Defense companies and their trade groups are pressing Congress not to require that the Pentagon approve stock buybacks.

items.title: Salesforce is on an AI buying spree, but Wall Street still has its doubts
items.content: The company has announced three deals in the month of June alone.

items.title: Japan is raising visa fees by up to 400% for the first time in nearly 50 years. Here's why
items.content: A single entry visa will climb to 15,000 yen, from the 3,000 yen charged currently, while multiple entry visas will cost 30,000 yen, up from the 6,000 yen now.

items.title: Waymo and Uber end robotaxi pilot in Phoenix
items.content: The Waymo self-driving cars deployed in Phoenix for the Uber pilot will remain in use and make autonomous deliveries with DoorDash.

items.title: Micron's monster post-earnings rally is almost gone. Traders divided on where it goes next
items.content: The maker of memory chips slid to start the week, marking an about-face from its post-earnings surge.

items.title: Supreme Court rulings on Fed, FTC: What they mean for consumers
items.content: The Supreme Court issued a pair of highly anticipated rulings on Monday that have far-reaching implications for household finances.

items.title: IRS says Trump Account contributions will not trigger annual gift tax reporting requirements
items.content: Parents, guardians and others who contribute to a Trump Account will not be required to file a gift tax return, the IRS and Treasury Department said Monday.

items.title: Oil prices near pre-war levels  —  but persistent supply risks could spark a rebound, analysts warn
items.content: Commodity strategists warn that Iranian leverage in the Strait of Hormuz will continue to weigh on oil markets and shipping costs.

items.title: Trump laments 'tremendous loss' on mail-in ballots at Supreme Court, doubles down on voter-ID bill
items.content: Justice Amy Coney Barrett, whom Trump nominated in 2020, rejected arguments that federal laws preempt Mississippi permitting late-arriving absentee ballots.

items.title: Comcast announces it will spin off NBCUniversal and Sky from cable business
items.content: Comcast said it will separate into two publicly traded companies through a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky.

items.title: Comcast's NBCUniversal spinoff raises hope for more deals. There may not be good options
items.content: Comcast plans to separate its cable and media divisions over the next year. This could set each company up for M&A, but there might not be any good options.

items.title: Oil prices rise as U.S. and Iran reach deal to halt attacks, U.S. oil above $70 per barrel again
items.content: Oil prices were higher after an agreement between the U.S. and Iran to halt recent hostilities in the Middle East.

items.title: This insurance stock is on an impressive run. How to ride the momentum with less risk
items.content: MetLife (MET) is well-positioned in the life insurance and benefits sector, exhibiting fundamental momentum that the broader market hasn't fully priced in.

items.title: Here's how Trump Accounts could affect women's retirement savings gap
items.content: While Trump Accounts are unlikely to directly improve retirement savings among women, there may be an indirect benefit, says one expert.

items.title: Kalshi traders expect this week's jobs report will disappoint Wall Street outlook
items.content: Dow Jones expects over 118,000 jobs will be added, but Kalshi traders give under 60% that over 100,000 jobs will be added.

items.title: Trump says U.S. and Iran to hold fresh talks in Qatar on Tuesday following weekend clashes
items.content: The U.S. and Iran are poised to hold fresh talks on Tuesday in Qatar's capital, President Donald Trump said via social media on Monday.

items.title: Putin’s fuel shortage admission signals growing strain on Russia’s energy infrastructure
items.content: Putin's comments mark the first time he has detailed the extent to which Ukraine's deep-strike successes have hampered Russia's fuel production.
